trivia_bullets.text: Battle for Zendikar (BFZ, released 2015-10-02) sets up the climax of the Eldrazi arc: Ulamog is defeated by the Gatewatch during BFZ, but Emrakul — the most powerful of the three Eldrazi titans — remains offscreen throughout the block. BFZ's Emrakul cards including Vestige of Emrakul reference her through her brood rather than her direct presence, building dread through absence. [Scryfall API; Wizards: MTG lore via WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text: "Emrakul has not been seen in months. Though her brood's numbers have dwindled in her absence, each drone is still a deadly threat." [Scryfall] Emrakul's absence is a deliberate narrative choice in BFZ — she appears only in Eldritch Moon (2016), set on Innistrad, having traveled through the Blind Eternities. The flavor text here functions as in-world foreshadowing: the threat level of individual drones is emphasized precisely because the titan herself is conserved for a more dramatic reveal.

A 3/4 Eldrazi Drone for {3}{R} with Devoid and Trample — vanilla-plus stats for common. Pauper-legal and paupercommander-legal. EDHREC rank 27,830 is the lowest of the wave-86 BFZ cards, consistent with its status as a pure beatstick with no synergistic text. [Scryfall]

Illustrated by Tyler Jacobson, a prominent MTG artist with landmark pieces including the Khans of Tarkir set's Sarkhan and multiple iconic planeswalker portraits. Jacobson's work on a common Eldrazi drone represents art-team investment in making even bulk commons visually arresting in BFZ's horror-invasion aesthetic. [Scryfall]
